text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Renata Kowalczyk
Medical Record Number: [MRN on file]
Ordering Provider: Hematology/Bone Marrow Transplant Service
Specimen Received: Bone marrow core biopsy and aspirate smears
Clinical Indication: Post-allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (alloHSCT) restaging; day +60 disease reassessment in a patient with history of acute myeloid leukemia (AML), FAB M4, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, DNMT3A R882H co-mutation; status post FluBu4 myeloablative conditioning and 10/10 MUD peripheral blood stem cell transplant.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Specimen ID: [Accession number on file]
Procedure Date: [Day +60 post-transplant]
Type of Specimen: Right posterior iliac crest core biopsy (one core, submitted in formalin) and bilateral aspirate smears (20 smears submitted, air-dried and fixed)
Submitting Service: Inpatient Bone Marrow Transplant Unit
Clinical History: 48-year-old female with de novo AML, FAB M4 (acute myelomonocytic leukemia), FLT3-ITD mutation (high allelic ratio, VAF 82%), DNMT3A R882H co-mutation, normal karyotype (46,XX), ELN 2022 Adverse risk. Achieved complete remission (CR) and MRD negativity after Cycle 1 induction with 7+3 (cytarabine/daunorubicin) plus midostaurin. Received one cycle of bridging IDAC consolidation. Underwent myeloablative alloHSCT (FluBu4 conditioning, ATG, tacrolimus/methotrexate GVHD prophylaxis) from a 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor (MUD), 38-year-old male. Day +28 peripheral blood chimerism: 99% donor. This biopsy is performed at day +60 for disease reassessment and engraftment confirmation.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
GROSS DESCRIPTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Received in formalin is a tan-pink cylindrical core biopsy of bone marrow measuring 1.6 cm in length and 0.2 cm in diameter. The core is intact, without fragmentation, and shows a uniform soft to slightly firm consistency on gross inspection. Sectioning reveals a homogeneous tan-red cut surface without discrete nodules, necrosis, or hemorrhagic foci identified grossly. The specimen is entirely submitted in one cassette following decalcification in 10% EDTA solution for 24 hours. Estimated adequacy: Adequate for diagnosis.

Also received are twenty (20) air-dried Wright-Giemsa-stained aspirate smears labeled with patient identifiers. Smears are of adequate cellularity with multiple spicular fragments identified. Representative smears selected for differential count (500-cell differential performed). Additional smears reserved for cytochemical stains and molecular send-out.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CORE BIOPSY (H&E-stained sections):
The core biopsy is well-preserved and adequate for evaluation. Overall cellularity is estimated at 55-65% (moderately cellular), which is appropriate and expected for the day +60 post-transplant interval, representing recovering engrafted donor hematopoiesis. The fat-to-hematopoietic ratio is approximately 40:60.

Trilineage hematopoiesis is confirmed with evidence of active maturation across all three lineages:

- Myeloid series: Myeloid precursors are present in orderly maturational sequence from promyelocytes through segmented neutrophils. No significant left shift or dysplasia is appreciated. No abnormal localization of immature precursors (ALIP) is identified.

- Erythroid series: Erythroid islands are distributed evenly throughout the biopsy. Normoblastic maturation is present without significant megaloblastic change. Occasional binucleate erythroid forms are noted but do not exceed the threshold for significant dyserythropoiesis. No ring sideroblasts are expected (iron stain pending, see below).

- Megakaryocytic series: Megakaryocytes are present in normal numbers with a range of sizes; predominantly mature forms with lobulated nuclei. No micromegakaryocytes, bare megakaryocyte nuclei clusters, or significant dysmegakaryopoiesis is identified. No megakaryocytic clustering along trabecular bone surfaces.

Blasts: Blasts constitute less than 2% of nucleated cells by visual estimation on core biopsy sections; no morphologically recognizable blast aggregates or sheets are identified.

Stroma: No fibrosis identified on reticulin stain (Grade MF-0, European Consensus Grading). No granulomata, necrosis, lymphoid aggregates of pathologic significance, or parasitized histiocytes identified. Hemosiderin deposits are present within macrophages in moderate amounts, consistent with prior transfusion support.

Vascular structures: Sinusoids are patent and non-dilated. No sinusoidal obstruction or fibrin thrombi identified.

ASPIRATE SMEARS (Wright-Giemsa):
Aspiration is adequate with multiple bone marrow spicules present. A 500-cell differential count was performed across multiple spicules:

  Myeloblasts:             1%
  Promyelocytes:           3%
  Myelocytes:             12%
  Metamyelocytes:         11%
  Band forms:             15%
  Segmented neutrophils:  17%
  Eosinophils (all stages): 3%
  Basophils:               1%
  Monocytes:               4%
  Promonocytes:            1%
  Erythroid precursors:   28%
  Lymphocytes:             3%
  Plasma cells:            1%
  Total blasts:            1% (below diagnostic threshold for AML)

The blast population present consists of small to medium cells with a modest nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio and inconspicuous nucleoli - these are consistent with normal residual hematopoietic progenitors and do not recapitulate the morphologic features of the original AML blasts (which showed high N:C ratio, prominent nucleoli, and irregular nuclear contours). NO Auer rods are identified. No monocytic blast population with the characteristics seen at diagnosis is identified.

Erythroid precursors show normoblastic maturation; no significant dyserythropoiesis. Iron stores are adequate on iron stain (see below). Megakaryocytes are seen on aspirate smears; forms are predominantly mature and lobulated.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
NO MORPHOLOGIC EVIDENCE OF RESIDUAL OR RECURRENT ACUTE MYELOID LEUKEMIA.

Bone marrow with trilineage hematopoietic reconstitution at day +60 post-allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation, moderately cellular (55-65%), blast count <2%, no dysplasia. Findings are consistent with successful engraftment and sustained complete morphologic remission.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ANCILLARY STUDIES
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
HISTOCHEMICAL STAINS (performed on core biopsy sections):
  - Reticulin stain (Gordon-Sweet): Grade MF-0 (no fibrosis). No increase in reticulin fibers above normal background.
  - Iron stain (Prussian blue): Moderate hemosiderin in macrophages; no ring sideroblasts identified on aspirate smears (0/100 erythroid precursors examined); iron stores adequate.
  - Periodic Acid-Schiff (PAS): No abnormal glycogen deposits in blast population.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (IHC, performed on formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded core biopsy):
  - CD34: Rare scattered positive cells, <1% of nucleated cells; no CD34+ blast aggregates or clusters; distribution is interstitial/sinusoidal consistent with normal hematopoietic progenitor distribution.
  - CD117 (c-KIT): Scattered positive cells corresponding to myeloid progenitors and mast cells; normal distribution, no abnormal clustering.
  - CD33: Diffuse positivity in myeloid series; normal maturational pattern.
  - MPO (myeloperoxidase): Positive in maturing myeloid elements; no aberrant MPO-negative blast population identified.
  - CD68 (PGM1): Scattered macrophages and monocytic elements; normal distribution.
  - CD3: Sparse T-lymphocytes present; no T-cell aggregates.
  - CD20: Rare B-lymphocytes; no B-cell aggregates or lymphoid follicles.
  - TdT: Negative; no TdT-positive lymphoblast population detected.
  - PAX5: Negative for lymphoid aggregates of concern.
  - Ki-67 (proliferation index): Approximately 15-20% in hematopoietic cellular areas; appropriate for recovering bone marrow; no focal areas of markedly increased proliferation.

FLOW CYTOMETRY IMMUNOPHENOTYPING (performed on aspirate, send-out to Immunophenotyping Laboratory - RESULT RECEIVED):
  Panel: 8-color multiparameter flow cytometry (MFC) for AML MRD assessment (CD45/CD34/CD117/HLA-DR/CD33/CD13/CD14/CD64/MPO).
  Blasts by CD45 dim/SSC gating: 0.8% of total nucleated cells (within normal limits).
  Aberrant immunophenotype (AIP) analysis: No immunophenotypically aberrant myeloid blast population identified. The CD34+/CD117+/HLA-DR+ compartment displays a normal antigen expression pattern without the CD13bright/CD33dim asynchrony or CD14/CD64 co-expression pattern characteristic of the original AML clone.
  MRD result: NEGATIVE. No evidence of measurable residual disease by MFC (sensitivity 0.01%; lower limit of detection 1 in 10,000 nucleated cells).
  Interpretation: Findings are consistent with normal hematopoietic recovery without residual leukemia.

MOLECULAR STUDIES (send-out, results received):
  1. FLT3-ITD by fragment analysis / real-time PCR:
     Result: UNDETECTABLE. No FLT3-ITD amplification products detected.
     Assay sensitivity: 10^-4 (1 in 10,000 cells).
     Interpretation: No detectable residual FLT3-ITD mutant clones.

  2. DNMT3A p.R882H by digital droplet PCR (ddPCR):
     Result: VAF 2.1% (low-level signal detected).
     Interpretation: Low-level DNMT3A R882H signal at VAF 2.1%. Given confirmed 99-100% donor chimerism at this timepoint, this low-level signal most likely represents residual host cells (approximately 1% residual host cells by chimerism) harboring the pre-existing CHIP clone rather than residual AML. This finding is stable compared to the day +28 post-induction assessment (VAF 1.8%). No intervention warranted. Continued monitoring recommended.
     NOTE: DNMT3A R882H mutations are among the most common CHIP mutations in the general population (estimated prevalence ~0.5% in individuals over 40) and the original pre-transplant clone likely represented a CHIP clone that was present antecedent to AML development. The co-existence of this signal at low VAF with complete donor chimerism on myeloid progenitors (see chimerism below) supports CHIP origin rather than AML persistence.

CHIMERISM ANALYSIS (performed on aspirate - results received from Molecular Pathology):
  Method: Short tandem repeat (STR) PCR analysis.
  Specimen: Unsorted bone marrow aspirate.
  Result: 99% donor / <1% host chimerism (unsorted marrow).

  CD34+ sorted progenitor chimerism (immunomagnetic bead selection prior to STR):
  Result: 100% DONOR chimerism in CD34+ compartment.
  Interpretation: Complete donor engraftment in the hematopoietic progenitor compartment. The trace host signal in unsorted marrow (<1%) is consistent with residual host stromal/non-hematopoietic elements.

CYTOGENETICS (conventional G-banding karyotype, performed on aspirate - result received from Cytogenetics Laboratory):
  Result: 46,XY [20/20]
  Interpretation: MALE karyotype identified in all 20 metaphases analyzed. This is CONSISTENT WITH COMPLETE DONOR ENGRAFTMENT given that the patient (Renata Kowalczyk) is female (46,XX at baseline) and the donor is a 38-year-old male. The presence of an exclusively male karyotype confirms complete replacement of host hematopoiesis by donor-derived cells. No cytogenetic abnormalities identified. No evidence of clonal cytogenetic evolution.

  FISH Panel (on aspirate interphase nuclei): Pending. [FLT3 locus deletion, inv(16), t(8;21) and TP53 FISH probes sent to outside reference laboratory; results to be addended upon receipt.]

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
COMMENT
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
This day +60 post-transplant bone marrow evaluation demonstrates sustained complete morphologic remission with no evidence of residual or recurrent acute myeloid leukemia. Hematopoiesis is trilineage and orderly, with cellularity appropriate for the post-transplant interval. The blast count is <2% by both morphology and flow cytometry immunophenotyping. MRD assessment by multiparameter flow cytometry is negative at high sensitivity (0.01%). The FLT3-ITD mutation, which served as the index molecular marker in this patient's AML, is undetectable by PCR (sensitivity 10^-4).

The cytogenetic finding of an exclusively male karyotype (46,XY) in this female patient provides definitive cytogenetic confirmation of complete donor engraftment, which is further substantiated by STR chimerism demonstrating 100% donor in the CD34+ progenitor compartment.

The low-level DNMT3A R882H signal (VAF 2.1%) detected by ddPCR is interpreted as consistent with residual host non-hematopoietic or stromal elements harboring a pre-existing CHIP clone, and not as evidence of AML persistence or recurrence. This interpretation is supported by: (1) undetectable FLT3-ITD (the driver mutation of the AML clone); (2) 100% donor chimerism in CD34+ progenitors; (3) absence of any morphologic or immunophenotypic evidence of AML; and (4) stability of the signal compared to prior assessment. Serial monitoring of this low-level signal is recommended.

Overall, these findings are highly favorable and consistent with deep molecular remission and complete donor engraftment at day +60 following myeloablative allogeneic HSCT for FLT3-ITD-mutant AML.

FISH panel results will be addended when available from the reference laboratory.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
DIAGNOSIS SUMMARY
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1. BONE MARROW, POSTERIOR ILIAC CREST, CORE BIOPSY AND ASPIRATE (day +60 post-alloHSCT):
   - COMPLETE MORPHOLOGIC REMISSION. No morphologic evidence of residual or recurrent acute myeloid leukemia.
   - Moderately cellular marrow (55-65%) with trilineage hematopoietic reconstitution; no dysplasia.
   - Blast count: <2% (morphology and flow cytometry; within normal limits).
   - MRD by multiparameter flow cytometry: NEGATIVE (sensitivity 0.01%).
   - FLT3-ITD by PCR: UNDETECTABLE (sensitivity 10^-4).
   - DNMT3A R882H by ddPCR: Low-level VAF 2.1%, interpreted as CHIP in residual host cells (see comment).
   - Cytogenetics: 46,XY [20/20] -- COMPLETE DONOR ENGRAFTMENT confirmed; no cytogenetic abnormalities.
   - Chimerism, CD34+ sorted: 100% DONOR.
   - No reticulin fibrosis (MF-0).
   - FISH panel: PENDING (addendum to follow).

Electronic signature on file.
Attending Hematopathologist: [Pathologist name on file]
Resident/Fellow: [Trainee name on file]
Date signed: [Date on file]
Report status: FINAL (with pending addendum for FISH results)
